steps.title: Compare the origins
steps.content: The passage says both words entered English through Spanish and both have Indigenous origins. The difference is the specific Indigenous source language: cocoa traces back to Nahuatl in Central Mexico, while alpaca traces back to Quechua in South America.

steps.title: Reject unsupported differences
steps.content: Choice A invents different times when the words entered English, but the passage gives no dates. Choice C claims that cocoa changed meaning while alpaca kept its original meaning, which the text does not discuss. Choice D adds a misconception about Spanish origin that the text never mentions.

steps.title: Choose the stated difference
steps.content: The remaining choice states the difference directly supported by the passage: cocoa can be traced to Nahuatl, while alpaca can be traced to Quechua. That is the contrast the text actually provides. The answer is B.
